metric_value: Aberdeen believes the time to implement object technology is now, when combined with appropriate levels of training and support
Successful IS executives achieve competitive objectives by developing flexible applications that model core organizational activities
Aberdeen's field research consistently shows OO development delivers real long-term benefits but only with the right mix of advanced tools and training
Object-oriented software is inherently modular resulting in better-organized code where problems and elements are more quickly isolated
Object-oriented applications can integrate existing software by building an interface wrapper around legacy systems, preserving previous software investments
An industry transition to distributed computing is underway driven by geographically distributed business units and the need to share core databases
notes: Section: Why Distributed Computing; Evidence: analyst-assertion

metric_value: When a business rule changes enterprise-wide it will become cost-prohibitive to have programming staff hand-code all occurrences without integrated distributed software methods
notes: Section: Why Distributed Computing; Evidence: analyst-assertion

metric_value: Aberdeen field research consistently shows that when any one of four critical factors is missing (robust tools / training / implementation expertise / leadership vendor) there is a much higher chance of delays, cost overruns, and user dissatisfaction
In Aberdeen's opinion, Hewlett-Packard has the right combination of products, services and partnerships needed in today's market
SoftBench has 80-plus third-party tools encapsulated into HP's development environment covering the entire software development lifecycle from analysis and design to testing and maintenance
Using the Encapsulator, SoftBench embeds third-party tools so they share the same user interface and seamlessly communicate with each other enabling best-of-breed tool selection
SoftBench supports not only HP-UX but also runs on Sun Solaris
SoftBench's static analyzer and debugger have gotten particularly high marks from developers interviewed by Aberdeen
SoftBench graphically depicts application structure and object inheritance, shortening the time needed for new developers to learn object technology
New SoftBench 5.0 Graphical Class Editor allows developers to visually edit objects with changes automatically reflected in underlying C++ code by making static analysis database read-write
Aberdeen believes CodeAdvisor represents an elegant way to teach new C++ developers the subtleties of OO programming and will significantly accelerate object adoption timetables
CodeAdvisor automatically enforces C++ coding rules by using the static analysis database to discover common coding errors such as improper object copying with dynamically allocated memory
SoftBench CM supports version control, checkin/checkout, audit trails, change histories and access control and allows parallel work across multiple HP-UX and Solaris servers
For systems mixing C++, C and COBOL, HP offers COBOL SoftBench and COBOL/C++ SoftBench enabling incremental migration from procedural to OO programming
SoftBench 5.0 supports Informix, DB2/9000, Oracle, Sybase and Allbase databases along with Micro Focus COBOL and CICS
SoftBench CM enables developers to collaborate on a project even when distributed across an enterprise
SoftBench can build applications that use OSF's Distributed Computing Environment enabling software modules to execute on multiple networked hardware platforms

Aberdeen believes SoftBench's team development features and ability to encapsulate third-party tools result in an excellent environment for professional developers building mission-critical applications
HP's experience shows customers first inventory existing applications to determine what should be encapsulated and linked to new OO applications using C++ SoftBench and UIM/X
Companies moving to object technology face two major challenges: development staff retraining and migrating the code base
Third-party libraries of generic pre-built objects and templates are used to provide base-level functionality reducing development time
HP offers a two-day basic bootcamp teaching SoftBench fundamentals tailored to programming languages and organizational needs
notes: Section: HP Consulting Services; Evidence: service-description

metric_value: HP provides focused on-site consulting helping clients design and implement distributed OO applications with project management expertise
notes: Section: HP Consulting Services; Evidence: service-description

metric_value: HP's Object-Oriented Solutions Center provides IS management organizational and business-related assistance including hardware platform selection, network design and software licensing
notes: Section: HP Consulting Services; Evidence: service-description

metric_value: HP assesses and provides services for organizational and training needs accompanying the transition to object technology
notes: Section: HP Consulting Services; Evidence: service-description

metric_value: HP provides Object-Oriented Distributed Computing Environment (OODCE) used in conjunction with SoftBench to create applications running on OSF DCE
HP Distributed Smalltalk (DST) allows building CORBA-compliant Smalltalk systems conforming to OMG CORBA specification using an object request broker
In the first half of 1996 HP plans to roll out ORB Plus to let developers create CORBA-compliant C++ applications with C++ SoftBench

Aberdeen firmly believes distributed computing will become increasingly critical to daily business operations and user organizations should evaluate a supplier's distributed computing strategy even with no immediate plans

HP's vision is applications assembled from pre-built components from internal development, third parties, or legacy systems; SoftBench will gain enhanced graphical programming and code maintenance capabilities
The static analysis database added in September 1994 SoftBench 4.0 rollout is the key underpinning for the newly-released SoftBench C++ CodeAdvisor and Graphical Editor demonstrating HP's forethought
HP is porting Taligent's CommonPoint framework to HP-UX; SoftBench can modify CommonPoint C++ components resulting in customized applications for collaborative desktop use
Aberdeen believes more object-adept SoftBench users will soon want to use CommonPoint to build collaborative desktop applications running on a wide range of hardware platforms
Aberdeen believes C++ SoftBench deserves serious attention from organizations contemplating a transition to object-oriented software development
HP is viewed as a trusted supplier with $25 billion in revenues having the resources to take on the largest and most complex assignments

Aberdeen believes organizations that realize the impact of the change in application development and take advantage of SoftBench's advanced features will be the first to turn information systems from a cost center into a revenue engine
The heavy use of objects by independent software vendors for developing products is accelerating the adoption of OO technology
A new development environment must include robust browsers, editors, compilers, support team development, and cover the full software lifecycle from analysis and design to testing
QuickFix automates the debugging process as one of the new features in SoftBench 5.0
SoftBench 5.0 includes a customizable color ToolBar for frequently used activities improving developer workflow
Aberdeen predicted more OO-adept SoftBench users would use CommonPoint to build collaborative desktop applications on wide hardware range
CommonPoint failed commercially due to extreme learning curve; Taligent dissolved into IBM January 1998; technology merged into Java efforts not C++ SoftBench path
notes: Source: Taligent Company History (wildcrest.com); Shadowrun.fr/taligent. CommonPoint sold very low volumes and was discontinued. Prediction incorrect.

metric_name: HP ORB Plus CORBA C++ rollout H1 1996
metric_value: HP planned to release ORB Plus in H1 1996 to enable CORBA-compliant C++ applications with C++ SoftBench
notes: Vendor roadmap claim from HP via Aberdeen study obs-33

metric_name: HP ORB Plus CORBA C++ rollout outcome
metric_value: HP did ship ORB+ and CORBA tools for HP-UX in 1996; however HP-UX and SoftBench ecosystem declined through late 1990s as Java and web-based development supplanted C++ toolchain; SoftBench eventually discontinued
notes: Source: Wikipedia SoftBench; CORBA was technically released but ecosystem did not achieve broad adoption. Partial: product shipped; ecosystem adoption did not materialize.

metric_name: Distributed computing critical to daily business
metric_value: Aberdeen firmly believed distributed computing would become increasingly critical to daily business operations
Distributed computing became foundational: client-server, web services, SOA, cloud computing all represent successive waves validating this prediction. Ubiquitous by 2000.
notes: Prediction confirmed. Internet boom, SOA, and cloud represent the realization of distributed computing imperative Aberdeen foresaw.

metric_name: OO technology adoption with right tools and training
metric_value: Aberdeen predicted that with right tools, training, expertise, and vendor support, OO development would deliver real long-term benefits; time to implement is now
notes: Core thesis of study; obs-01, obs-03, obs-08

metric_value: OO development became mainstream through late 1990s via Java (1995), C++ wide adoption, Python. However the specific C++/HP-UX SoftBench path was displaced by Java, Eclipse, and open source IDEs by 2000.
notes: Direction correct (OO became universal) but specific HP SoftBench platform did not dominate. Partial success.
